channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korean law enforcement officials enter presidential compound to detain impeached Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hundreds of law enforcement officials in South Korea entered the residential compound of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol early Wednesday in the capital Seoul. It was their second attempt to detain him over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prominent Puerto Rican civil rights leader José “Cha Cha” Jiménez dies at 76
item.description: José “Cha Cha” Jiménez, a prominent civil rights and liberation movement figure and founder of the Young Lords in Chicago and co-founder of the Rainbow Coalition has died. He was 76. His sister, Daisy Rodríguez, said in a Facebook post that he died Friday morning. A cause of death was not given.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: These 2 Democrats voted for House Republicans' bill to bar transgender athletes from girls sports
item.description: Reps. Vicente Gonzalez and Henry Cuellar, both of Texas, were the only Democrats to side with Republicans on Tuesday to pass legislation meant to bar transgender athletes from competing in girls and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Biden moves to lift state sponsor of terrorism designation for Cuba, part of deal to free prisoners
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden notified Congress of his intent to lift the U.S. designation of Cuba as a state sponsor of terrorism, the White House announced, as part of a deal facilitated by the Catholic Church to free political prisoners on the island. Senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New wildfire warnings issued and more power is shut off as winds rise in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Millions Southern Californians faced new wildfire warnings Tuesday, and tens of thousands saw their power shut off as strong winds blew across the parched landscape around Los Angeles where two massive blazes have been burning for a week.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Octavia Butler imagined LA ravaged by fires. Her Altadena cemetery survived
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — Decades ago, the writer Octavia Butler had imagined a Los Angeles ravaged by fires. The Altadena cemetery where the science fiction and Afrofuturism author is buried did catch fire last week but suffered “minimal damage,” according to a statement on the cemetery's web site.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and CHRIS PIZZELLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawyers for Sean 'Diddy' Combs say sex acts are distorted by 'sexist and puritanical' prosecution
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs labeled the prosecution against the hip-hop star as “sexist and puritanical” on Tuesday, saying nine recordings of sex acts considered important evidence in the case show only consensual sex between willing adults.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing persons: New Ohio task force to reunite families with loved ones
item.description: Ohio is taking a significant step to change how missing persons cases are handled. Governor Mike DeWine has announced the formation of a new task force designed to improve investigations and reunite families with their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Chemicals in sewage sludge fertilizer pose cancer risk, EPA says
item.description: Harmful chemicals in sewage sludge that is spread on pasture land as fertilizer are causing cancer, the Environmental Protection Agency said Tuesday. The risk is highest for people who regularly consume milk, beef and other products from farms where it is spread.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: A surprising AP photograph of an undamaged Volkswagen van surrounded by destruction from a Los Angeles wildfire has astonished viewers.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Orleans Police can begin the process of ending federal oversight
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The New Orleans Police Department can begin ending its longstanding federal oversight, a judge ruled Tuesday in response to a request from the city and the Justice Department to wind down monitoring.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Federal prosecutors won't seek charges in deadly arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene
item.description: Federal prosecutors told family members Tuesday they will not bring charges in the deadly 2019 arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene, closing the books on a lengthy FBI investigation into the white troopers who stunned, punched and dragged Greene on a roadside and allegations of an attempted cover-
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Incident commanders say field damage inspections are ongoing and may change as new information is gathered and verified.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Berkeley Amazon employee stabbed, killed by co-worker: Police
item.description: Upon arrival, they located 31-year-old Kenneth Anderson-Lane, who was stabbed once following an "altercation with a co-worker," the release noted.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Alex Murdaugh, accomplice ordered to pay millions in money scheme after housekeeper's death
item.description: A South Carolina federal judge ordered Alex Murdaugh and his accomplice, Cory Fleming, to pay millions after they conspired to steal from Gloria Satterfield's family.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAA investigating after United, Delta flights nearly collide before landing at Phoenix airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration is investigating after a United Airlines flight and a Delta Airlines flight had a near-collision over Phoenix on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth grilled over comments on women serving in combat
item.description: The start to hearings for President-elect Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees kicked off on Tuesday with Pete Hegseth who is hoping to become the next secretary of defense.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Police hunt 'Haggler After Midnight' mugger targeting certain victims in bizarre crime spree
item.description: The "Haggler After Midnight," who has struck nearly a dozen times in Brooklyn and Queens, forces victims to haggle at knifepoint over what loot they will hand over, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP incumbents brace for MAGA primary challengers
item.description: Several GOP Senate incumbents are already staring down the threat of primary challenges next year as friction builds between the MAGA and establishment wings. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Rashida Jones is stepping down as MSNBC president on the eve of Trump inauguration
item.description: MSNBC President Rashida Jones says she is stepping down after four years leading the liberal news network, her move coming on the eve of a second Trump administration and after changes in corporate ownership.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blue city judge slammed for releasing violent suspect arrested again for vicious attack on teen
item.description: Seattle Judge Veronica Galvan released an armed robbery suspect who then allegedly viciously attacked a 14-year-old in what critics say is a soft-on-crime issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wildfires latest: Firefighters prepare for dry conditions and strong winds
item.description: Firefighters around Los Angeles were preparing on Tuesday to attack flare-ups or new blazes. The National Weather Service issued a rare warning that dry winds combined with severely dry conditions created a “ particularly dangerous situation," indicating that any new fire could explode in size.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Small airborne embers play a big role in the spread of wildfires
item.description: While authorities still don’t know what sparked the deadly fires in the Los Angeles area, they do know one clear way the flames have spread: embers. At least 24 people have died in the fires that have destroyed more than 12,000 structures since starting last Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's Cabinet pick confirmation hearings set to begin and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Multiracial boom in 2020 census was mostly an illusion, researchers say
item.description: When the 2020 census results were released, they showed a boom in the number of people classified as multiracial in the United States since 2010. Two Princeton sociologists now say that jump was mostly an illusion.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sexual predator sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah, planning to meet minor
item.description: A Florida man has been sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah and planning to have sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Heidi Montag tops iTunes chart after losing house in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Reality television star Heidi Montag's 2010 album “Superficial” has hit No. 1 on iTunes' all-genre songs and albums charts — 15 years after its release and just after Montag and husband Spencer Pratt lost their home in the Palisades Fire.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anita Bryant, singer known for opposing gay rights, dies at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant, a former Miss Oklahoma, Grammy-nominated singer and prominent booster of orange juice and other products who became known over the second half of her life for her outspoken opposition to gay rights, has died. She was 84.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump flies US flag at full height before end of 30-day mourning period following Carter's death
item.description: U.S. flags at President-elect Donald Trump 's private Mar-a-Lago club are back to flying at full height.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UFC champion booted from Frontier flight after being told he couldn't sit in exit row
item.description: Former UFC lightweight champion Khabib Nurmagomedov was removed from a Frontier Airways flight on Saturday after refusing to state whether he would be able to assist other travelers in the event of an emergency, a spokesperson for Frontier told Nexstar on
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4
